compiled_state.title: As a validation process, I can export Figma design frames as PNG with rate-limit awareness
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Exports Figma design frames as PNG via REST API with rate-limit awareness, caching, and resolution normalization to match the comparison viewport.
**Why**: Provides the baseline design image that the perceptual diff compares against the implemented UI.
**Scope**:
- IN: GET /v1/images/:key with format=png, lastModified check before re-export, 3-5s delay between sequential requests, local PNG cache, resolution normalization to configured viewport
- OUT: Playwright screenshots (S2), perceptual diff scoring (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, Figma frames are exported as PNG via REST API with a lastModified check that skips re-export if the Figma file hasn't changed since last cache
  ← Satisfies: "checks lastModified before re-exporting"
- When this story is complete, sequential image export requests include a 3-5 second delay to stay within rate limits
  ← Satisfies: "rate-limit-aware client"
- When this story is complete, exported PNG dimensions match the configured viewport (design.figma_viewport, default 1440x900) via scale factor computation or post-export resize; output path convention: designs/{uuid}/validation/<frame_node_id>.png
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding on resolution contract + output naming
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] GET /v1/images/:key 429 after ~10 sequential requests — cache + lastModified check critical
- [Performance] Resolution normalization: Figma frames may be designed at arbitrary canvas sizes; exported PNG must match Playwright viewport for pixelmatch to work (pixelmatch requires identically-sized images)
- [Integration] Verified: GET /v1/images/:key supports format=png, scale parameter, returns temporary download URL

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
